title: T-031 — API: Variance tracking nightly job (catch-weight)
prompt:
# T-031 — API: Variance tracking nightly job (catch-weight)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §8.1, §8.5, §8.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Nightly variance computation job; emits alert when avg variance exceeds threshold.

Provide a migration creating catch_weight_variance_daily table with (org_id, item_id, day, avg_variance_pct, stddev, samples). Tests stub work_order_items rows. Alert outbox event 'catch_weight.alert'.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_catch_weight_variance_daily.sql [create]
- lib/cron/catch-weight-variance.ts [create]
- tests/wiring/catch-weight-variance.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Scale HW driver
- Variance UI

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given 100 weighings with avg variance 4%, threshold 5%, when job runs, then no alert emitted.
2. Given avg variance 7%, when job runs, then 'catch_weight.alert' present in outbox with item_id+avg.
3. Given fixed-weight items, when job runs, then they are skipped (no rows produced).
4. Given empty work_order_items, when job runs, then no errors (graceful no-op).

## Test strategy
- RED tests for threshold behaviour + skip rules.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/catch-weight-variance.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not modify work_order_items (read-only).
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
